response:
To compute the **sample variance** of the data set:

$$
\{9, 14, 5, 4, -20, -13, -5, 13\}
$$

---

### Step 1: Find the mean (average) of the data

$$
\text{Mean} = \frac{9 + 14 + 5 + 4 + (-20) + (-13) + (-5) + 13}{8}
$$

$$
= \frac{9 + 14 + 5 + 4 - 20 - 13 - 5 + 13}{8}
$$

$$
= \frac{14}{8} = 1.75
$$

---

### Step 2: Subtract the mean from each data point and square the result

$$
\begin{align*}
9 - 1.75 &= 7.25 \quad \Rightarrow \quad (7.25)^2 = 52.5625 \\
14 - 1.75 &= 12.25 \quad \Rightarrow \quad (12.25)^2 = 150.0625 \\
5 - 1.75 &= 3.25 \quad \Rightarrow \quad (3.25)^2 = 10.5625 \\
4 - 1.75 &= 2.25 \quad \Rightarrow \quad (2.25)^2 = 5.0625 \\
-20 - 1.75 &= -21.75 \quad \Rightarrow \quad (-21.75)^2 = 473.0625 \\
-13 - 1.75 &= -14.75 \quad \Rightarrow \quad (-14.75)^2 = 217.5625 \\
-5 - 1.75 &= -6.75 \quad \Rightarrow \quad (-6.75)^2 = 45.5625 \\
13 - 1.75 &= 11.25 \quad \Rightarrow \quad (11.25)^2 = 126.5625 \\
\end{align*}
$$
